zh-tw.title: 廢話文學語錄
zh-tw.description: 一句話講了很多，但什麼都沒說——廢話文學精選，讀完只能點頭稱是

en.title: Absurd Wisdom
en.description: Statements that sound profound until you realize they're stating the obvious — curated nonsense that somehow makes total sense

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學格式參考，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
科學家發現，每當一分鐘過去之後，那一分鐘就不在了。
目前沒有任何方法可以改變這個結果。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳到群組引發困惑，也很適合當作跟朋友說廢話的開場白
variations: 每當時鐘走完一個小時，你就已經少了六十分鐘的人生。這是事實。

en.content:
Scientists have confirmed that once a minute has passed, that minute is no longer present.
No method has been found to reverse this.
en.editorNote: Drop this in a group chat to cause confusion — or use it to open a conversation about absolutely nothing
variations: Every time the clock completes an hour, sixty minutes of your life are gone. This is not a drill.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學下雨傘例句，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
帶了傘的人，在下雨的時候通常不會淋濕。
沒有帶傘的人，結果恰好相反。

這個現象，在有傘和沒有傘的人身上都得到了驗證。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下雨天傳給沒帶傘的朋友，效果絕佳
variations: 出門前記得看天氣預報，因為不看的話，你就不知道今天的天氣是什麼。

en.content:
People who bring an umbrella tend to stay dry when it rains.
Those without an umbrella experience the opposite.

This phenomenon has been observed in both groups.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who forgot their umbrella — maximum effect guaranteed
variations: Check the weather forecast before you leave. Without checking it, you won't know what the weather is.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學食物例句，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
研究指出，當一個人把盤子裡的食物全部吃完之後，
盤子裡就不會再有食物了。

除非再裝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給說「我在節食」又一直夾菜的朋友，畫面感滿分
variations: 吃太飽的人，比吃剛好的人，多吃了一些。這是真的。

en.content:
Research indicates that after a person finishes all the food on their plate,
there will no longer be food on the plate.

Unless more is added.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who says they're dieting but keeps eating — perfectly timed
variations: A person who overeats has eaten more than a person who ate just enough. This is accurate.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學年齡例句，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
你今年幾歲，就代表你已經活了幾年。
這中間沒有例外，也沒有辦法有例外。
zh-tw.editorNote: 生日的時候傳給朋友，比任何生日祝福都更讓他停下來想一下
variations: 過了今年，你就比今年大一歲了。比去年也大了一歲。這是每年都會發生的事。

en.content:
However old you are is precisely how many years you have been alive.
There are no exceptions to this, nor can there be.
en.editorNote: Send on someone's birthday — it'll make them pause more than any regular birthday message
variations: After this year, you'll be one year older than you are this year. This happens every year.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學睡眠觀察，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
一個人在睡覺的時候，通常是因為他沒有醒著。
醒著的人，則通常還沒睡著。

這兩種狀態，每個人都體驗過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的時候看最有共鳴，傳給說「我睡不著」的朋友也很合適
variations: 睡不著的人，在他睡著之前，都會一直睡不著。

en.content:
When a person is asleep, it is generally because they are not awake.
People who are awake, conversely, have not yet fallen asleep.

Everyone has experienced both states.
en.editorNote: Perfect for 3am insomnia or to send to a friend who says they can't sleep
variations: A person who can't sleep will continue to be unable to sleep until they fall asleep.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學財務知識，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
花掉的錢，就不在你的口袋裡了。
沒花的錢，還在。

這兩種情況，根據你有沒有花錢來決定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 月底窮到沒錢的朋友看了最有感，或傳給那個一直說要存錢卻一直花錢的人
variations: 你花了多少錢，你就少了多少錢。這個關係是直接的。

en.content:
Money that has been spent is no longer in your possession.
Money that has not been spent remains.

Which of these applies depends on whether you have spent the money.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who's broke at the end of the month — or to the one who keeps saying they'll save but never does
variations: However much money you spend, that is exactly how much less money you have. The relationship is direct.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學運動健身觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
體重計顯示的數字，
無論你站上去幾次，都是你目前的體重。

站更多次，不會讓數字變小。
但很多人還是會再站一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 健身房或節食中的朋友看了最有感，傳過去保證有共鳴
variations: 反覆量體重只是讓你更頻繁地知道你的體重，而不是改變它。

en.content:
The number shown on a scale represents your current weight,
regardless of how many times you step on it.

Stepping on it more often will not make the number smaller.
And yet, most people step on it again.
en.editorNote: Send to any friend on a diet or going to the gym — they'll feel personally attacked
variations: Weighing yourself repeatedly only gives you more frequent access to information you already know.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學天氣觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
根據長期觀察，晴天的特徵，是沒有在下雨。
下雨天的特徵，是雨正在落下。

陰天則介於兩者之間，但更靠近其中一種。
zh-tw.editorNote: 天氣不好的時候傳給朋友，比任何氣象預報更精準地什麼都沒說
variations: 今天天氣很熱，是因為現在的溫度比較高。這個分析是準確的。

en.content:
Long-term observation shows that sunny days are characterized by an absence of rain.
Rainy days are characterized by rain falling.

Cloudy days fall somewhere between the two, though closer to one than the other.
en.editorNote: Send when the weather is bad — more accurate than any forecast while saying absolutely nothing
variations: Today is hot because the temperature is high. This analysis is correct.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學歷史觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
所有發生在很久以前的事情，
都可以稱為過去的事情。

而那些還沒發生的事，目前仍然沒有發生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 歷史課作業的開頭用這段，老師可能會愣住；傳給唸歷史系的朋友，他會懂
variations: 昨天的事情，在今天來看，都是昨天發生的。這個角度是正確的。

en.content:
Everything that happened a long time ago
can be referred to as something that happened in the past.

Things that haven't happened yet remain, at this time, unhappened.
en.editorNote: Open your history essay with this — the teacher might pause; send to a history major for full effect
variations: Yesterday's events, viewed from today, all took place yesterday. This perspective is accurate.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學人際關係觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
兩個陌生人，在成為朋友之前，
曾經有過一段互相不認識的時期。

那段時期，就是他們還不是朋友的時候。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給新認識的朋友，說「我們以前還不是朋友」，既準確又沒什麼意義
variations: 認識你之前，我不認識你。認識之後就認識了。這個過程在大部分友誼中都存在。

en.content:
Before two strangers become friends,
there exists a period during which they do not know each other.

This period is defined as the time before they became friends.
en.editorNote: Tell a new friend 'before we knew each other, we didn't know each other' — accurate and entirely pointless
variations: Before I knew you, I didn't know you. Afterward, I did. This process exists in most friendships.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學旅行觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
如果你一直往前走，
你就會離你出發的地方越來越遠。

但如果你走回來，距離就縮短了。
不走的話就不會有以上任何一種情況。
zh-tw.editorNote: 規劃旅行的時候發現自己看了半天還沒訂票，可以傳給自己看
variations: 到達目的地，代表你已經在目的地了。出發前則不在。

en.content:
If you keep walking forward,
you will find yourself increasingly far from where you started.

If you walk back, the distance decreases.
If you don't move, neither happens.
en.editorNote: Relatable when you've been 'planning' a trip for three hours without booking anything
variations: Arriving at a destination means you are now at the destination. Before departing, you were not.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學人生哲學，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
人生就是這樣：
有時候順，有時候不順。

順的時候，是因為事情比較順利。
不順的時候，則恰好相反。
兩種情況都有可能出現。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友在抱怨的時候傳這個，你既表示了理解，又沒有給任何實質建議
variations: 生活有好的時候，也有不好的時候。這兩者都叫做生活。

en.content:
Life is like this:
Sometimes things go well, and sometimes they don't.

When things go well, it's because they are going well.
When they don't, it's the opposite.
Both outcomes are possible.
en.editorNote: Send when a friend is venting — you've acknowledged their situation while offering zero actual advice
variations: Life has good parts and not-so-good parts. Both of these are still life.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學學習觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
不懂的東西，在你搞懂之前，你是不懂的。
搞懂之後，就懂了。

這個過程，叫做學習。
不學習的話，這個過程就不會發生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 考試前傳給在說「我完全不懂」的朋友，看他是要生氣還是笑出來
variations: 你越努力，就越接近你努力的目標。不努力，則離得遠一些。

en.content:
The things you don't understand remain things you don't understand — until you understand them.
After that, you understand them.

This process is called learning.
Without learning, this process does not occur.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who says 'I have no idea what I'm doing' before an exam — 50/50 chance of laughter or anger
variations: The harder you work, the closer you get to what you're working toward. Less work means further away.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學手機使用觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
手機沒電的時候，
螢幕會變黑，無法使用。

充電之後，電池裡就有電了。
插著充電器但沒有插電，效果有限。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個問你「為什麼我手機沒電了」的人，精準但沒用
variations: 如果手機的電量是零，你就需要讓它不是零，才能繼續使用。

en.content:
When a phone has no battery,
the screen goes dark and the device becomes unusable.

After charging, the battery will contain charge.
Plugging in a charger that is not connected to power has limited effect.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who asks 'why is my phone dead?' — precise, accurate, and completely unhelpful
variations: If your phone battery is at zero, it will need to be not at zero before you can use it again.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學社群媒體觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
發限時動態之後，
看過的人，就看過了。
沒看的人，沒看到。

二十四小時後，限時動態消失。
但有沒有人看過，已經是事實。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個很在意限動觀看數的朋友，讓他安靜地重新評估人生
variations: 你發了文，沒有人按讚，代表目前沒有人按讚。這可能之後會改變，也可能不會。

en.content:
After posting a story,
people who have seen it have seen it.
People who haven't seen it have not.

After twenty-four hours, the story disappears.
But who saw it or didn't — that is already a fact.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend obsessively checking their story views — give them a moment to quietly reconsider life
variations: If you post something and nobody likes it, it means nobody has liked it yet. This may or may not change.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學閱讀觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
一本書，在你把它讀完之前，
是一本你還沒讀完的書。

讀完之後，就成了你讀完的書。

如果你根本沒讀，它就是你沒讀的書。
三種狀態，取決於你讀了多少。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給書架上有一堆沒讀完的書的朋友，他會默默照一張書架的照片給你
variations: 你的書籤停在哪一頁，那本書就從那一頁開始等你。它一直在等。

en.content:
Before you finish a book,
it is a book you have not yet finished.

After you finish it, it becomes a book you have finished.

If you never read it at all, it is a book you have not read.
All three states depend on how much you have read.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend with a shelf full of unfinished books — they'll silently photograph their nightstand
variations: Your bookmark marks the exact page where the book began waiting. It is still waiting.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學排隊現象觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
在排隊的時候，
你前面的人，都比你更早到達隊伍。

你後面的人，則是在你之後加入的。

當你排到最前面，就輪到你了。
這個規則在大多數排隊場合都成立。
zh-tw.editorNote: 在排長龍的地方傳給旁邊的人，既說了廢話，又建立了陌生人之間的奇妙連結
variations: 插隊的人不在這個分析範圍內，因為他們選擇了不同的人生路徑。

en.content:
When standing in a queue,
the people in front of you arrived before you did.

The people behind you joined the queue after you.

When you reach the front, it is your turn.
This rule applies in most queuing situations.
en.editorNote: Share with whoever's next to you in a long line — technically correct and a great icebreaker
variations: People who cut in line are not included in this analysis. They have chosen a different path.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學朋友聯絡觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你的手機聯絡人清單裡，
有一些人你已經很久沒有傳訊息了。

在那段期間，你們之間的訊息數量為零。

這個情況，在雙方都沒有傳訊息的情況下，會繼續持續。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給某個很久沒聯絡的朋友，當作重新開啟對話的破冰神器
variations: 按下傳送之後，就不再是零了。這是改變現狀最有效的方法。

en.content:
In your phone contacts,
there are people you have not messaged in a very long time.

During that period, the number of messages exchanged between you was zero.

This situation will continue for as long as neither party sends a message.
en.editorNote: Send this to a friend you've lost touch with — the perfect absurd icebreaker to restart a conversation
variations: Once you press send, it is no longer zero. This is the most effective method for changing the current situation.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學動物觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
科學家觀察到，貓咪在睡覺的時候，通常是沒有清醒的。

清醒的貓咪，則通常不在睡覺。

這兩種狀態的共同點是：牠都不在理你。
無論你怎麼叫，現狀都不會改變。
zh-tw.editorNote: 養貓的人看了會覺得這是精準的科學報告，而不是廢話
variations: 狗的情況相反：無論清醒還是半夢半醒，牠都在理你。這個現象目前沒有例外。

en.content:
Scientists have observed that a cat, while sleeping, is generally not awake.

A cat that is awake is, conversely, not asleep.

Both states share one characteristic: the cat is not paying attention to you.
This does not change regardless of how many times you call its name.
en.editorNote: Cat owners will recognize this not as nonsense, but as accurate scientific reporting
variations: Dogs show the opposite pattern: awake or half-asleep, they are always paying attention to you. No exceptions have been recorded.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學已讀未回觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你傳了一則訊息。
對方看了，但目前沒有回覆。

在他回覆之前，你處於「已讀未回」的狀態。

這個狀態將持續，直到它不再持續。
目前沒有其他可能。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在等回訊的朋友，他看了可能會笑，也可能更焦慮，視情況而定
variations: 如果你不回覆，對方處於等待狀態。如果你回覆，等待狀態結束。這是唯一的解法。

en.content:
You sent a message.
They read it, but have not yet replied.

Until they reply, you are in the state of having been read but not answered.

This state will continue until it no longer continues.
No other outcomes are currently available.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend anxiously waiting for a reply — they'll either laugh or feel slightly worse, depending on the situation
variations: If you don't reply, the other person is waiting. If you do reply, the waiting ends. This is the only known solution.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學樂觀悲觀哲學觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
半杯水，就是一個杯子裡裝了一半的水。
另一半沒有水。

樂觀的人說：杯子還有一半。
悲觀的人說：杯子只剩一半。

無論哪種說法，水的量都沒有改變。
這是兩種人唯一的共識。
zh-tw.editorNote: 哲學討論開始前可以用這個暖場，對方通常會沉默兩秒然後問你到底想說什麼
variations: 把水喝掉的人不在這個討論範圍之內，他已經跳出了這個問題。

en.content:
A glass half full of water is a glass with water in half of it.
The other half has no water.

The optimist says: the glass is still half full.
The pessimist says: the glass is already half empty.

Regardless of interpretation, the amount of water has not changed.
This is the only point of agreement between the two.
en.editorNote: Use this to open a philosophical discussion — your audience will pause for two seconds and then ask what you're actually trying to say
variations: The person who drinks the water is outside the scope of this analysis. They have exited the problem entirely.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學計畫執行觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
制定了計畫的人，目前有一個計畫。
沒有制定計畫的人，目前沒有計畫。

執行計畫的人，正在執行那個計畫。
沒有執行的人，計畫尚未被執行。

這四種情況，在任何時間點都至少有一種在發生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個說「我有計畫，但還沒開始執行」的朋友，讓他重新審視自己目前在哪一格
variations: 計畫本身不會變成行動。行動是另一個步驟，在計畫結束之後才開始。

en.content:
A person who has made a plan currently has a plan.
A person who has not made a plan currently does not have a plan.

A person executing their plan is in the process of executing it.
A person not executing their plan has a plan that is not yet being executed.

At any given moment, at least one of these four states applies to you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who has 'a plan but hasn't started yet' — invite them to identify which of these four boxes they're in
variations: A plan does not automatically become action. Action is a separate step that begins only after the plan ends.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學開會觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
參加會議的人，在會議進行中，是在會議裡的。
沒有參加的人，則不在裡面。

會議結束之後，與會者離開會議室。
在離開之後，他們就不在會議室裡了。

這個流程，在每次會議結束後都會發生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下次在會議室裡說這段，看有幾個人沉默兩秒然後點頭表示贊同
variations: 在會議中說話的人，比沒有說話的人，多說了一些話。這是可以驗證的。

en.content:
People attending a meeting are, while the meeting is in progress, inside the meeting.
People who are not attending are not inside it.

After the meeting ends, attendees leave the meeting room.
Once they have left, they are no longer in the meeting room.

This sequence of events occurs at the conclusion of every meeting.
en.editorNote: Read this out loud in a meeting room and count how many people nod in serious agreement
variations: People who spoke during the meeting said more things than those who didn't. This can be verified.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學飢餓觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
根據長期研究，人在餓的時候，通常處於非飽的狀態。
吃東西之後，飢餓感會有所減輕。

吃太多的情況下，可能會超過飽的程度，進入撐的狀態。

這三種狀態通常按照上述順序出現，但也有人跳過第二種，直接進入第三種。
zh-tw.editorNote: 吃到一半說這個，旁邊繼續夾菜的人會假裝沒聽見，因為他正在越過第二種狀態
variations: 「我不餓」這句話，在進入餐廳三分鐘後，通常會被「好像可以吃一點」取代。這是常見現象。

en.content:
Long-term research confirms that a person who is hungry is, at that time, not full.
After eating, the level of hunger decreases.

In cases of overeating, fullness may be exceeded, entering the state of being stuffed.

These three states generally occur in the order listed, though some individuals skip the second and proceed directly to the third.
en.editorNote: Say this mid-meal — whoever keeps reaching for more food will pretend not to hear, because they're actively skipping state two
variations: 'I'm not hungry' typically transitions into 'maybe I could eat a little' within three minutes of entering a restaurant. This is a well-documented pattern.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學遺忘觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你忘記的事，在你忘記之後，就已經被你忘記了。

你記得的事，目前仍在你的記憶中。

兩者的根本差異，在於你是否還記得。
如果你已經忘記了某件事，你通常不知道你忘記了。
這就是忘記的核心機制。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個說「我完全想不起來」的朋友，讓他面對忘記這件事的本質，然後繼續想不起來
variations: 你以為自己記性好的那些事，可能只是你還沒忘記。尚未忘記的事和記住的事，感覺是一樣的。

en.content:
The things you have forgotten are, after being forgotten, things you no longer remember.

The things you still remember are currently present in your memory.

The fundamental difference between these two categories is whether you remember them.
If you have already forgotten something, you are typically unaware that you have.
This is the core mechanism of forgetting.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who says 'I can't remember at all' — a thorough examination of what forgetting actually means
variations: The things you believe you remember well may simply be things you haven't forgotten yet. Unforgotten things and remembered things feel identical.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學運動習慣觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
開始運動的人，比還沒開始的人，早了一步開始運動。

尚未開始的人，目前仍在開始之前。
兩者之間的差距，在其中一方開始之後，會縮減為零。

但「下週一開始」的人，在下週一來臨之前，仍然屬於尚未開始的那一類。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每週日晚上都說「明天開始運動」的朋友，讓他安靜地接受自己的狀態分類
variations: 計畫運動的人和實際運動的人，目前分屬兩個不同的集合。這兩個集合，偶爾有重疊。

en.content:
A person who has started exercising has begun exercising earlier than someone who hasn't started yet.

The person who has not yet started is currently still in the pre-start phase.
The gap between them will reach zero once one of them begins.

However, a person who intends to 'start on Monday' remains in the not-yet-started category until Monday actually arrives.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who says 'I'm starting to exercise next week' every Sunday — let them quietly accept their current classification
variations: People who plan to exercise and people who actually exercise currently belong to two distinct categories. These categories occasionally overlap.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學接住情緒觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
如果你一直接住所有人的情緒，
請記得偶爾也要讓自己落地。

一直在空中接球的人，
也是從某個地方起跑才有辦法接的。

大部分的人要等到落地才想到這一點。
但這也是事實。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個習慣照顧所有人情緒、卻很少被問「你還好嗎」的人——是廢話，也是一種提醒
variations: 接住了很多人，不代表你不需要被接住。這兩件事可以同時為真。

en.content:
If you are always catching other people's emotions,
remember to let yourself land occasionally.

Someone who is always in the air catching things
had to run from somewhere to get there in the first place.

Most people only realize this after they've already hit the ground.
But this is also a fact.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who always takes care of everyone else and is rarely asked 'are you okay?' — technically obvious, genuinely meaningful
variations: Catching many people does not mean you don't need to be caught. Both of these things can be true at the same time.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學喜歡一個人觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
喜歡一個人這件事，
通常在你已經喜歡上之後，你才會察覺到。

察覺之前，你通常不確定自己有沒有喜歡。
察覺之後，你會後悔為什麼沒有早點確定。

但在察覺之前，你確實是不確定的。
這個過程在很多人身上都發生過，目前沒有例外。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給正在「搞不清楚自己什麼心情」的朋友，讓他用廢話文學的角度重新看待自己的感情狀態
variations: 當你開始在意對方有沒有傳訊息給你，通常代表你已經在意了。在那之前你以為你沒在意。這兩個階段都是真實的。

en.content:
The experience of liking someone
is usually only noticed by you after you already like them.

Before noticing, you are generally unsure.
After noticing, you wonder why it took you so long to figure it out.

But before that moment, you were genuinely unsure.
This process has occurred in many people, with few known exceptions.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who is 'not sure what they're feeling' — reframes their emotional state through absurd-wisdom logic
variations: When you start checking if they've texted you, you have already started caring. The period before you noticed? You still cared. You just didn't know yet.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學情緒好轉觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
感覺好一點，通常是在你感覺不好一段時間之後才發生的。
感覺不好的時候，還沒感覺好一點。

但根據目前的紀錄，感覺不好的狀態通常不是永久的。
雖然在感覺不好的當下，這個結論不太容易被接受。

以上是目前的觀察結果。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給正在心情不好的朋友，比任何安慰的話都更不令人反感，但本質上說的是同一件事
variations: 根據數據顯示，大部分人在心情不好之後，最終都進入了心情比之前稍好的狀態。這個趨勢目前仍然成立。

en.content:
Feeling better usually happens after a period during which you did not feel better.
While you are not yet feeling better, you have not yet felt better.

However, based on available records, the state of not feeling well is generally not permanent.
This conclusion is admittedly difficult to accept while you are still in that state.

These are the current findings.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend having a rough time — less intrusive than actual comfort, but says exactly the same thing underneath
variations: Data suggests that most people who were not feeling well eventually entered a state of feeling somewhat better. This trend remains consistent.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學大甲媽祖遶境觀察，重新創作，限時（4/17–4/26）

zh-tw.content:
根據長期觀察，
跟隨媽祖遶境完整走完九天八夜的人，
比只走了一部分的人，多走了一些路。

在這九天八夜之中，時間持續流逝。

遶境結束之後，遶境就結束了。
這個現象，每年在固定的時間都會再次發生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 大甲媽祖遶境起駕期間傳出去，宗教盛事也可以是廢話文學的完美素材——說了很多，但什麼都沒說，而且完全是事實
variations: 補充觀察：沒有跟著走的人，在那九天八夜裡，也沒有走完全程。這個差異，在兩組人之間都得到了驗證。

en.content:
Long-term observation confirms
that those who complete all nine days of the Mazu pilgrimage
have walked more distance than those who completed only part of it.

During these nine days, time continues to pass.

After the pilgrimage concludes, it is over.
This phenomenon recurs each year at approximately the same time.
en.editorNote: Share during the Mazu pilgrimage season — even sacred cultural events are not immune to absurd wisdom. Everything stated is accurate. Nothing of substance has been said.
variations: Additional finding: those who did not participate also did not complete the full distance during those nine days. This difference has been confirmed in both groups.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學截止日期觀察，參考職場幽默語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
在截止日期之前完成的工作，
是在截止日期之前完成的。

在截止日期之後完成的，則超時了。

研究發現，很多工作在「截止日期前最後幾小時」完成。
在那之前，這些工作通常處於「尚未完成」的狀態。
這個現象，在幾乎每個有截止日期的人身上都能觀察到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 期末或交件前一天傳給還在拖的朋友，他不會謝謝你，但這是事實
variations: 提早完成的人，比截止日期更早完成了工作。他們目前比較少在緊張。

en.content:
Work completed before the deadline
was completed before the deadline.

Work completed after the deadline was late.

Research shows that a significant portion of work gets finished in the final hours before the deadline.
Prior to that, it was in the state of not yet being finished.
This pattern has been observed in nearly every person who has ever had a deadline.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend still procrastinating the night before something is due — they won't thank you, but every word is true
variations: People who finish early completed their work before those who finished late. They are currently experiencing less stress.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學密碼忘記觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你忘記密碼的那個帳號，
在你想起密碼之前，你是登不進去的。

點了「忘記密碼」之後，系統會傳一封信給你。
那封信，在你去收信之前，你還沒收到。

重設完密碼之後，你就有新密碼了。
那個新密碼，你通常在下次登入時就又忘了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 每次換密碼都忘的人看了最有共鳴，傳給那個又在說「我忘記密碼了」的朋友
variations: 設定「好記的密碼」的人，通常在設定完之後就忘了為什麼這個密碼是好記的。

en.content:
The account whose password you have forgotten
cannot be accessed until you remember the password.

Clicking 'forgot password' will cause the system to send you an email.
That email has not yet been received by you until you check your inbox.

After resetting your password, you will have a new password.
This new password is typically forgotten again at the next login attempt.
en.editorNote: Anyone who forgets passwords regularly will feel seen — send to the friend who just said 'I forgot my password again'
variations: People who set 'easy to remember' passwords typically forget why the password was supposed to be easy to remember shortly after setting it.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學自拍照片觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
拍了二十張自拍之後，
你擁有二十張自拍。

其中有幾張你會覺得還可以，
剩下的你通常會刪掉。

刪掉的那些，在刪掉之後，就不在你的手機裡了。
留下來的那張，就是你最後發出去的那張。
這個篩選流程，在每次自拍時都會重新執行一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個拍照超久、精修後才發的朋友，精準描述他的整個工作流程
variations: 覺得自己沒有上相的人，通常是在拍了很多張之後，才選出一張比較上相的。這不是矛盾，這是流程。

en.content:
After taking twenty selfies,
you have twenty selfies.

A few of them will seem acceptable.
The rest you will typically delete.

The deleted ones, once deleted, are no longer on your phone.
The one you keep is the one you end up posting.
This filtering process runs again from the beginning each time you take selfies.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who spends ten minutes taking photos before posting one — an accurate step-by-step description of their entire workflow
variations: People who think they're not photogenic usually find one acceptable photo only after taking many. This is not a contradiction. It is a process.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「要吃什麼」決策疲勞觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
「要吃什麼」這個問題，
在你決定之前，是一個尚未被回答的問題。

回答之後，問題就有了答案。

但根據觀察，部分人在回答之後，
又對答案感到懷疑，重新進入未決定狀態。
這個循環，在某些群組討論中可以持續超過四十分鐘。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳到任何一個在討論「要吃什麼」的群組，保證立即引發沉默或爆笑，兩者都算成功
variations: 說「都可以」的人，和說「不要吃那個」的人，目前對這個問題貢獻了方向相反的資訊。

en.content:
The question 'what should we eat'
remains an unanswered question until someone answers it.

Once answered, the question has an answer.

However, observation shows that some individuals, after an answer is reached,
begin to doubt the answer and re-enter the undecided state.
This loop has been documented in certain group chats lasting over forty minutes.
en.editorNote: Drop this into any group chat debating where to eat — it will cause either immediate silence or uncontrollable laughter, both count as a win
variations: The person who says 'anything is fine' and the person who says 'not that place' are currently contributing information in opposite directions.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學 AI 使用觀察，參考 Fortune 2026 年報導，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
使用 AI 的人，目前在使用 AI。
沒有使用的人，目前沒有在使用。

AI 回答了問題之後，那個問題就有了一個 AI 的回答。
至於那個回答對不對，
需要知道答案的人才能判斷。

不知道答案的人，通常就是問 AI 的那個人。
這個現象，目前還沒有完美的解決方案。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個問完 AI 又來問你「這樣對嗎」的朋友，讓他靜靜想一下剛才發生了什麼
variations: 讓 AI 確認 AI 答案的人，正在用同一個來源驗證同一個來源。這個方法目前仍被廣泛使用。

en.content:
People who are using AI are currently using AI.
People who are not using it are not.

After AI answers a question, that question has an AI-generated answer.
Whether the answer is correct
can only be judged by someone who already knows the answer.

The person who doesn't know the answer is typically the one who asked AI in the first place.
No perfect solution to this situation has yet been identified.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who asks AI something and then asks you 'is this right?' — let them quietly reflect on what just happened
variations: Using AI to verify an AI answer means using the same source to check the same source. This method remains widely practiced.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學咖啡因依賴觀察，參考 Snugfam 生活幽默觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
喝了咖啡的人，比沒喝咖啡的人，多喝了一杯咖啡。

咖啡因進入身體之後，就在身體裡了。
在它的效果消退之前，效果是存在的。
消退之後，部分人會再喝一杯。

這個循環，在有咖啡可以喝的情況下，通常會繼續發生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個說「我不喝咖啡會沒辦法工作」的人，精準描述他的每一天，但完全沒有提供任何解方
variations: 沒喝到咖啡就頭痛的人，在喝到咖啡之前，頭是痛的。喝完之後，通常就不痛了。這個結果是可以預期的。

en.content:
A person who has had coffee has consumed one more cup of coffee than a person who hasn't.

Once caffeine enters the body, it is inside the body.
Its effect exists until it wears off.
After it wears off, some people have another cup.

This cycle tends to continue as long as coffee remains available.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who says 'I can't function without coffee' — an accurate account of their daily life with zero solutions offered
variations: People who get a headache without coffee have a headache until they drink coffee. After drinking it, the headache typically goes away. This outcome is predictable.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學手機通知觀察，參考 Snugfam 準確幽默語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
手機收到通知的時候，通知就到了。
還沒收到的通知，目前還沒到。

看了通知的人，已經看了那則通知。
沒看的人，通知對他們來說還是未讀的。

已讀通知的數量，和你點開來看的次數，呈現正相關。
目前沒有其他方式可以增加這個數字。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個通知數字一直堆到幾百則、卻說「我等一下再看」的人——這個分析完全正確，也完全沒有幫助
variations: 關掉通知的人，不會收到通知。開著通知的人，在通知來的時候會收到。這個差異，取決於設定。

en.content:
When a phone receives a notification, the notification has arrived.
Notifications that haven't arrived yet have not yet arrived.

People who have looked at a notification have seen that notification.
For those who haven't looked, it remains unread.

The number of read notifications is directly proportional to how many times you've opened them.
No other method of increasing this number currently exists.
en.editorNote: Send to the person with 400 unread notifications who keeps saying 'I'll check later' — entirely accurate, entirely unhelpful
variations: People who turn off notifications don't receive notifications. People who leave them on do — when a notification arrives. The difference comes down to settings.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學通勤觀察，參考可預測預言文章，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
上班的人，在抵達公司之前，通常還在路上。

搭捷運的人，在捷運上。
開車的人，在車裡。
走路的人，在走路。

三種人抵達公司之後，都不在路上了。
這個結果，和他們各自使用的交通工具無關。
zh-tw.editorNote: 通勤中看到這個，會有一種被精準觀察卻又沒有任何資訊量的奇妙感——適合傳給同事，讓他知道你也在路上
variations: 比預定時間早到公司的人，比準時到的人更早抵達。比預定時間晚到的人，則晚了。這三種人，每天早上都會出現。

en.content:
People on their way to work are, before arriving at the office, still on the way.

Those taking the subway are on the subway.
Those driving are in their car.
Those walking are walking.

All three groups are no longer on the way once they arrive.
This outcome is unrelated to which mode of transportation they used.
en.editorNote: Read this on your commute — there's a unique feeling of being precisely observed while learning absolutely nothing. Perfect to forward to a coworker so they know you're both en route
variations: People who arrive early got there before people who arrived on time. People who arrived late got there after. All three types show up every morning.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學超市購物決策觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
進超市之前，你有一份購物清單，或者你沒有。

有清單的人，在超市裡通常還是會買一些不在清單上的東西。
沒有清單的人，在超市裡完全依靠直覺。

兩種人離開超市的時候，
都帶著一些他們進去之前沒有的東西。

這個現象，在超市有促銷的情況下，會更加明顯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個「我只是去買一樣東西」結果提了一大袋回來的人，這個分析解釋了他剛才發生了什麼
variations: 買了比需要更多的人，目前擁有比需要更多的東西。這個狀態，在東西用完之前，會持續存在。

en.content:
Before entering the supermarket, you either have a shopping list or you don't.

People with a list typically still buy several things that aren't on it.
People without a list operate entirely on instinct.

Both groups leave the supermarket
carrying things they didn't have when they walked in.

This phenomenon becomes more pronounced when the supermarket is running a sale.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who went in for 'just one thing' and came out with a full bag — this analysis explains exactly what happened to them
variations: People who bought more than they needed currently own more than they needed. This state will persist until the items are used up.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學「明天再做」拖延觀察，參考幽默生活智慧語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
說「明天再做」的人，
今天沒有做那件事。

明天到來之後，那個人可能做了，也可能再說一次「明天再做」。

持續說「明天再做」的人，
會在每一個今天都沒有做那件事。

直到有一天，那天的明天來不及了。
這個結局，在事後回看，通常是可以預見的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個已經說了三週「明天再做」的人，這是目前最準確也最沒用的提醒
variations: 「等我有空再做」的人，和「明天再做」的人，目前屬於同一個集合。這個集合非常大。

en.content:
A person who says 'I'll do it tomorrow'
did not do it today.

When tomorrow arrives, that person may do it — or may say 'I'll do it tomorrow' again.

Someone who keeps saying 'I'll do it tomorrow'
will not have done it on any of the todays leading up to that point.

Until one day, tomorrow is too late.
In hindsight, this outcome is usually one that could have been seen coming.
en.editorNote: Send to someone who has been saying 'I'll do it tomorrow' for three weeks straight — the most accurate and most useless reminder currently available
variations: 'I'll do it when I have time' and 'I'll do it tomorrow' belong to the same category. That category is very large.

items.sourceNote: 接住 slang; QuickSeek data: 284,309 Threads/IG mentions, #2 trending Taiwan slang 2026; original 廢話文學 format

zh-tw.content:
如果你習慣接住所有人的情緒，
記得偶爾也要讓自己落地。

一直在空中的東西，
最後都會很累。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「接住」是2026台灣最熱門的網路用語之一，代表接住別人的情緒和不安。這句廢話文學說的是顯而易見的事，但確實讓人心頭一緊。適合「留友看」傳到Threads上。
variations: 接住了所有人，卻忘了接住自己——這不叫善良，這叫還沒學會說不。

en.content:
If you're used to catching everyone else's emotions,
remember to let yourself land sometimes.

Something that's always in the air
eventually gets very tired.
en.editorNote: The concept of 'catching' someone's emotions (接住) is one of Taiwan's top trending phrases of 2026. This is 廢話文學 at work — stating the obvious in a way that actually lands. Good for sharing on Threads with '留友看.'
variations: Catching everyone else but forgetting yourself — that's not kindness. That's not knowing how to say no yet.

items.sourceNote: 硬控 slang from gaming culture; 完全被captivated; original 廢話文學 format per 2026 slang research

zh-tw.content:
有些人把你硬控了，
是因為你一直在想著他們。

不是因為他們特別，
是因為你給了他們特別的位置。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「硬控」來自遊戲術語，指被完全控制住、動彈不得。這句廢話文學說的其實是很顯而易見的道理，但讀起來反而有點道理。適合傳給那個說「不知道為什麼就是很在乎」的朋友。
variations: 你以為是被硬控了，其實只是你主動交出了遙控器。

en.content:
Some people completely captivate you
because you keep thinking about them.

Not because they're special —
because you decided to give them a special place.
en.editorNote: '硬控' comes from gaming — it means being completely controlled with no way to move. This 廢話文學 states something obvious but somehow lands with unexpected weight. Send to a friend who says 'I don't know why I can't stop thinking about them.'
variations: You think you've been captivated by someone. Actually you just handed them the remote control.

items.sourceNote: 甄嬛傳 absurdist wisdom angle; 2026 viral wave; original 廢話文學 format applying palace drama logic to modern life

zh-tw.content:
看完甄嬛傳才明白：

每個人說話之前，
都至少有三個可能的動機。

所以你每次聽到別人說話，
其實都只聽到了二十五分之一。

剩下的，叫做揣摩。
zh-tw.editorNote: 宮廷劇廢話文學。這句話說的是非常顯而易見的事——人類溝通確實複雜——但包裝成甄嬛傳才能說出這種既視感。適合傳給和你一起追劇的朋友。
variations: 職場版：開會時每個人說的話，背後都有三到五種解讀。學甄嬛，先不要反應，先想三秒。

en.content:
After watching palace dramas, you finally understand:

Every time someone speaks,
there are at least three possible motivations.

So every time you hear someone talk,
you've only understood one twenty-fifth of it.

The rest is called reading between the lines.
en.editorNote: Palace drama 廢話文學. States something obvious — human communication is complicated — but packaging it in palace drama logic gives it that 'surprisingly profound' feeling. Send to someone you're watching dramas with.
variations: Office version: in every meeting, each thing someone says has three to five interpretations. Study Zhen Huan — don't react immediately. Wait three seconds.

items.sourceNote: 後悔決策觀察，參考 Goodreads 荒謬主義語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
後悔一個決定的人，
是因為做了那個決定。

後悔沒做某件事的人，
是因為當時沒做。

兩種人都在後悔，
只是後悔的方向不一樣。

目前沒有一種決定，可以同時避免這兩種後悔。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在糾結「當初應不應該」的朋友，精準描述了人類決策的根本困境，但完全沒有提供任何出口
variations: 做了之後後悔，和沒做之後後悔，兩者都叫後悔。選哪一種，是唯一可以決定的事。

en.content:
A person who regrets a decision made that decision.

A person who regrets not doing something didn't do it at the time.

Both are experiencing regret.
The only difference is the direction.

No decision currently exists that avoids both kinds of regret simultaneously.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend stuck in 'what if I had / hadn't' — accurately diagrams the fundamental trap of human decision-making while offering absolutely no way out
variations: Regretting something you did and regretting something you didn't do are both called regret. Choosing which kind is the only choice available.

items.sourceNote: 天氣抱怨觀察，參考哲學幽默文章，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
根據觀察，
幾乎每個人都談論過天氣。

談論天氣的人，並沒有因此改變天氣。
天氣在被談論之後，繼續按照它原本的計畫進行。

這個現象，在晴天、雨天和陰天都得到了驗證。
談論天氣的人數，目前仍在持續增加中。
zh-tw.editorNote: 任何天氣都可以傳，傳完對方也還是會繼續抱怨天氣，這也是一個已知現象
variations: 說「今天好熱」的人和說「今天好冷」的人，對天氣的看法不同，但對天氣的影響力相同——都是零。

en.content:
Observation shows that
almost everyone has talked about the weather at some point.

People who talk about the weather do not change the weather by doing so.
After being discussed, the weather continues according to its original plan.

This has been confirmed on sunny days, rainy days, and cloudy days alike.
The number of people talking about the weather continues to grow.
en.editorNote: Works in any weather — and after you send it, the recipient will continue complaining about the weather, which is also a documented phenomenon
variations: The person who says 'it's so hot today' and the person who says 'it's freezing' have different opinions about the weather but equal influence over it — which is zero.

items.sourceNote: 手機螢幕使用時間觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
每週，你的手機會告訴你，你這週花了多少時間看手機。

你是用手機看到這則通知的。

看完之後，
你的螢幕使用時間增加了一點點。
這個結果，是無法避免的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 每週日收到螢幕使用時間報告的時候傳出去，效果最佳；閱讀這則廢話的行為本身，也會讓你的使用時間再多一秒
variations: 對使用時間感到震驚然後繼續滑手機的人，下週會收到一份類似的報告。這個循環目前沒有已知的中斷機制。

en.content:
Every week, your phone tells you how much time you spent looking at your phone.

You read this notification on your phone.

After reading it,
your screen time has increased by a small amount.
This outcome cannot be avoided.
en.editorNote: Best sent on Sunday when the weekly screen time report arrives — reading this piece of absurd wisdom also adds a few more seconds to your total
variations: People who feel shocked by their screen time and then keep scrolling will receive a similar report next week. No known mechanism for breaking this cycle has been identified.

items.sourceNote: 理髮後不滿意觀察，參考幽默生活觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
剪完頭髮不滿意的人，
目前有一個他不滿意的髮型。

好消息是：頭髮會長回來。
壞消息是：長回來之後，你還是要再剪一次。

再剪一次，又有可能不滿意。
這個循環，在頭髮持續生長的情況下，將無限重複。
zh-tw.editorNote: 剪完出來跟你說「剪壞了」的朋友，需要的不是安慰，是這段話——它什麼忙都幫不上，但至少說清楚了問題的本質
variations: 滿意的人和不滿意的人，最終都會再去剪一次。這是唯一確定的結局。

en.content:
A person who is unhappy with their haircut
currently has a haircut they are unhappy with.

The good news: hair grows back.
The bad news: once it grows back, it will need to be cut again.

The next cut may also result in dissatisfaction.
This cycle will repeat indefinitely as long as hair continues to grow.
en.editorNote: When a friend exits the salon saying 'it's ruined' — this won't help at all, but it does clearly explain the nature of the problem
variations: People who are satisfied and people who are dissatisfied will both need another haircut eventually. This is the only guaranteed outcome.

items.sourceNote: 網路購物購物車觀察，參考 Snugfam 生活幽默觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
你把商品加入購物車，
代表商品目前在你的購物車裡。

沒有結帳的購物車，裡面放的東西，還沒有變成你的。

等到你回去看，可能發現商品已經售完，
或者你已經不那麼想要了。

不管哪一種情況，購物車還是空了。
這兩種結局，在加入購物車的當下都是可能的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個購物車常常塞了幾十樣東西卻一個都沒結帳的朋友，精準描述他的購物車的宿命
variations: 收藏了很多沒有買的東西的人，擁有一份很長的「可能清單」。這份清單，和他的實際家當，目前不重疊。

en.content:
Adding an item to your cart means the item is currently in your cart.

A cart that has not been checked out contains things that are not yet yours.

By the time you return, the item may be sold out,
or you may no longer want it quite as much.

Either way, the cart ends up empty.
Both outcomes were possible from the moment you added the item.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose cart is permanently full of things they never buy — an accurate forecast of what their cart's future holds
variations: A person who has saved many items without buying them owns a very long list of possibilities. This list and their actual possessions currently do not overlap.

items.sourceNote: 鬧鐘貪睡觀察，參考幽默拖延語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
設了七點鬧鐘的人，
通常不會在七點起床。

他們會在七點時，把鬧鐘延後十分鐘。
然後在七點十分，再延後十分鐘。

這個流程，有時候會重複四到六次。

最後起床的時間，
和當初設鬧鐘的時間，通常相差五十分鐘以上。

設鬧鐘這個動作，因此主要是心理上的保障，而非實際的起床機制。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個說「我設了五個鬧鐘」但還是每天遲到的朋友，這是對他生活模式最精確的學術分析
variations: 鬧鐘響的時候，按掉的人，比起床的人，多睡了一些。這個選擇，在每個鬧鐘響起時都會重新做一次。

en.content:
A person who sets a 7am alarm
generally does not wake up at 7am.

At 7am, they will postpone the alarm by ten minutes.
At 7:10am, they will postpone it again.

This process sometimes repeats four to six times.

The actual wake-up time
typically differs from the original alarm time by fifty minutes or more.

The act of setting an alarm is therefore primarily a source of psychological reassurance, rather than a functional wake-up mechanism.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who sets five alarms and still shows up late — this is the most precise academic analysis of their morning routine available
variations: A person who snoozes the alarm has chosen to sleep longer than a person who got up. This decision is made fresh each time the alarm goes off.

items.sourceNote: 激勵自己但沒有行動觀察，參考幽默激勵語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
看了很多激勵人心的內容的人，
目前看了很多激勵人心的內容。

看完之後感到很有動力的人，
擁有一段感到有動力的時間。

那段時間結束之後，
有些人開始行動，有些人繼續尋找下一個激勵來源。

兩種人目前都處於某種狀態，
只是狀態的內容不同。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個看了一下午勵志影片、結果什麼都沒做的人，這不是批評，這只是觀察
variations: 感到有動力和實際開始行動，是兩件事。有時候它們會同時發生，有時候不會。這個統計數字，目前沒有公開資料。

en.content:
A person who has consumed a lot of motivational content
has, at this point, consumed a lot of motivational content.

Someone who feels inspired after watching it
possesses a period of feeling inspired.

Once that period ends,
some people take action; others seek out the next source of motivation.

Both groups are currently in some kind of state.
The content of that state differs.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who spent all afternoon watching motivational videos and did nothing afterward — this is not judgment. This is observation.
variations: Feeling motivated and actually starting something are two separate events. Sometimes they happen together. Sometimes they don't. Statistics on this are not publicly available.

items.sourceNote: 小組分工觀察，參考幽默日常語錄，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
小組作業分工之後，
負責做事的人，正在做事。
沒有在做的人，目前沒有在做。

到了交件前一天，
一直沒有在做的人，通常會開始詢問進度。

詢問進度這個動作，
本身並不等於貢獻進度。

這個差異，在每次小組作業中都可以觀察到，
但在下一次組隊時通常不會被納入考量。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個整個學期沒出現、交件前一天突然很積極的組員——精準，且毫無建設性，這很廢話文學
variations: 五個人的小組，有時候只有兩個人在做事。另外三個人的存在，對小組人數的貢獻，和對成果的貢獻，是不對等的。

en.content:
After dividing tasks in a group project,
the people doing work are doing work.
The people not doing work are currently not doing work.

On the day before the deadline,
those who have not been working typically begin asking for progress updates.

Asking for a progress update
is not the same as contributing to progress.

This distinction can be observed in every group project,
but is rarely factored in when forming the next group.
en.editorNote: Send to the group member who disappeared all semester and became suddenly very engaged the night before the deadline — precise, constructive in no way, and fully in the spirit of absurd wisdom
variations: In a group of five, sometimes only two people are doing the work. The remaining three contribute equally to the headcount and unequally to the outcome.

items.sourceNote: 接住情緒廢話文學，原創

zh-tw.content:
如果你總是接住所有人的情緒，
記得偶爾也要讓自己落地。

不然你接得住全世界，
卻沒有人接住你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是照顧別人情緒、忘記照顧自己的朋友；聽起來很有道理，但其實只是說了一個很明顯的事
variations: 接了別人的情緒，要記得找個地方放。目前科學上沒有發現情緒的存放位置，但這個問題確實存在。

en.content:
If you're always catching everyone else's feelings,
remember to let yourself land somewhere too.

Otherwise, you hold up the whole world —
but there's no one holding you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's always the emotional support for everyone else — it sounds profound, but it is simply pointing out something quite obvious
variations: After catching other people's emotions, you need somewhere to put them. Science has not identified an emotional storage location, but the problem remains real.

items.sourceNote: 初級大人廢話文學，基於2026 Threads viral trend，原創

zh-tw.content:
你知道什麼叫做初級大人嗎？

就是已經可以獨立繳水電費、付房租，
但芹菜要怎麼炒，
還是要打電話問媽媽的那種人。

這是一種被社會認定為成年，但廚房不認可的狀態。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給假裝自己已經完全獨立，但其實廚房技能仍在更新中的朋友
variations: 初級大人可以簽合約、辦貸款，但不確定味噌湯要放幾匙味噌。這兩件事沒有衝突，只是還沒解決。

en.content:
A junior adult is a person who is capable of paying utilities and rent independently,
but still requires a phone call to their mother
before attempting to cook celery.

This describes a state of being legally recognized as an adult
while remaining unrecognized by the kitchen.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who claims full independence but texted their mom about pasta this week
variations: Junior adults can sign contracts and apply for loans, but are uncertain how many spoonfuls of miso go in miso soup. These facts coexist without conflict.

items.sourceNote: 大甲媽祖遶境廢話文學，2026時事，原創

zh-tw.content:
跟著媽祖走9天，
科學研究顯示，
你的腳，一定會痛。

沒有其他可能的結果被觀測到。
zh-tw.editorNote: 大甲媽祖遶境期間（4月中至4月底）傳出去效果最好；純廢話文學格式，只說了一件很明顯的事
variations: 走340公里的結果是：你走完了340公里。這個結果和距離完全相符。

en.content:
Walking for nine days in a religious procession,
scientific observation confirms:
your feet will hurt.

No alternative outcomes have been recorded.
en.editorNote: Works best during Taiwan's Mazu Pilgrimage season (mid to late April) — pure absurd wisdom format, stating only the very obvious
variations: The result of walking 340 kilometers is: you have walked 340 kilometers. This outcome is fully consistent with the distance.

items.sourceNote: 治癒系廢話文學，跨topic格式，原創

zh-tw.content:
治癒系語錄說：接住你的情緒。

廢話文學問：
接完之後放哪裡？

目前尚未發現合適的情緒存放點。
研究仍在進行中。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合同時訂閱療癒語錄和廢話語錄的人傳給彼此，兩種世界的交叉點
variations: 接住情緒之後，情緒依然存在，只是換了一個人持有。持有者的感受，目前沒有統一的處理方式。

en.content:
Healing quotes say: catch your feelings.

Absurd wisdom asks:
And then put them where?

No suitable emotional storage location has been identified.
Research is ongoing.
en.editorNote: For anyone who subscribes to both healing content and absurd wisdom — this is the intersection
variations: After catching someone's emotions, those emotions still exist — they've just changed hands. No standardized method for processing them has been established.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學奶茶省錢觀察，參考廢話文學格式，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
只要每天省下買一杯奶茶的錢，
十天後，你就有十天份的奶茶錢。

但你也有十天沒喝到奶茶了。

這兩件事，目前沒有辦法同時成立。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個說「我要開始存錢，從戒奶茶開始」的朋友——這句話把他的兩難說清楚了，但完全沒有幫助他做決定
variations: 省錢的人，比沒省錢的人，少花了一些錢。但也少享受了一些。這個交換，每個人的評價不同。

en.content:
If you skip buying one bubble tea every day,
after ten days you'll have saved exactly ten days' worth of bubble tea money.

You will also have gone ten days without bubble tea.

These two facts cannot both be favorable at the same time.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who says 'I'm going to start saving money — no more bubble tea' — this accurately maps their dilemma while offering zero help in resolving it
variations: A person who saves money has spent less than a person who didn't. They have also enjoyed less. How each person weighs that trade-off varies.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學就是這樣觀察，參考同義反覆幽默，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
這件事就是這樣。

在它變成另一種樣子之前，
它就是它目前的樣子。

接受這件事的人，已經接受了。
還沒接受的人，目前還在接受的過程中，或者還沒開始。

無論如何，這件事還是這樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 人生遇到任何無解的狀況時都可以傳，因為它說的是任何狀況都適用的道理——也就是什麼都沒說
variations: 「就是這樣」這句話，在說完之後，事情還是這樣。這個結果，和你說不說這句話無關。

en.content:
It is what it is.

Before it becomes something else,
it is exactly what it currently is.

People who have accepted this have accepted it.
People who haven't are either in the process of accepting it, or haven't started yet.

Regardless, it is still what it is.
en.editorNote: Works for any unsolvable situation — because it applies to every situation, which means it applies to none in particular
variations: Saying 'it is what it is' does not change what it is. The outcome is unrelated to whether the phrase is spoken.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學筆電充電觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
把充電線插上筆電，
但沒有插上插座的人，
正在進行一個充電的姿勢，
但沒有在充電。

電池的電量，在這個情況下，會持續減少。

這個現象，在忘記插電的人身上，每天都有機會發生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個筆電沒電卻說「我有插啊」的人，有時候問題的答案比你想的更單純
variations: 插著充電線但沒有連接電源的筆電，和沒有插充電線的筆電，目前擁有相同的充電速度。

en.content:
A person who has plugged the charger into their laptop
but not into the wall
is performing the motion of charging
without actually charging.

The battery level will continue to decrease in this situation.

This phenomenon has the opportunity to occur every day in people who forget to check the wall end.
en.editorNote: Send to the person whose laptop died even though they 'had it plugged in' — sometimes the answer is simpler than expected
variations: A laptop connected to a charger that isn't plugged into a power source has the same charging rate as a laptop with no charger at all.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學群組訊息已讀觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
在群組裡傳了一則訊息的人，
傳了一則訊息到那個群組。

群組裡的人，可能看到了，也可能沒看到。

看到但沒有回應的人，
目前處於「看到了但沒有回」的狀態。
這個狀態和個人回覆意願高度相關。

那個沒有人回應的訊息，
現在靜靜地停在那裡。
這也是一種存在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳入任何一個沉默的群組，效果最佳；它說的是每個人都懂的群組生態，但完全無助於讓群組活起來
variations: 群組裡說話的人，比沒有說話的人，說了更多話。但群組的活躍程度，不只取決於說了多少話。

en.content:
A person who sent a message in a group chat
has sent a message to that group chat.

Members of the group may or may not have seen it.

People who saw it but didn't reply
are currently in the state of having seen it but not replied.
This state is strongly correlated with personal willingness to respond.

The message with no replies
is sitting there quietly right now.
This too is a form of existence.
en.editorNote: Drop into any silent group chat for maximum effect — it describes the group dynamic everyone recognizes while doing absolutely nothing to revive the conversation
variations: The person who talks in a group chat has said more than people who haven't said anything. Whether the group feels active depends on more than just how much was said.

items.sourceNote: 廢話文學帶傘沒下雨觀察，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
帶了傘出門，結果沒下雨。

很多人會覺得：是因為我帶了傘，所以沒下雨。

但根據目前的氣象研究，
傘對天氣的影響力，
和沒帶傘的人對天氣的影響力，
是一樣的。

兩者都是零。
這個結果，不影響人們繼續相信帶傘能防雨的說法。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下雨前傳給那個說「帶傘出去就不會下雨了」的朋友，讓他靜靜面對自己的邏輯——它聽起來很有道理，但只是一個巧合
variations: 沒帶傘就下雨的人，和帶了傘沒下雨的人，對天氣的控制能力相同。只是其中一個比較濕。

en.content:
You brought an umbrella and it didn't rain.

Many people conclude: it didn't rain because I brought an umbrella.

However, current meteorological research shows
that the influence of an umbrella on the weather
is equal to the influence of not bringing one.

Both are zero.
This finding has not stopped people from continuing to believe that carrying an umbrella prevents rain.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who says 'if I bring an umbrella it definitely won't rain' — let them quietly examine their own logic. It sounds reasonable. It is a coincidence.
variations: The person who forgot their umbrella and got rained on, and the person who brought one and stayed dry, have equal influence over the weather. One of them is just wetter.
